channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain sounds missile alert sirens as Iran vows retaliation for US strikes
item.description: Bahrain activated its missile alert system as Iran announced it would retaliate after the US launched new airstrikes. The tiny Gulf kingdom hosts the US Fifth Fleet and is within range of Iranian missiles.

item.title: 🇵🇰 At least 15 killed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir clashes
item.description: Thousands of protesters clashed with security forces in Pakistan-administered Kashmir over reserved seats for refugees. At least 11 civilians and 4 security officers were killed in the violence.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu to seek re-election despite Trump doubts
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu will run for re-election this year, his party announced, after US President Donald Trump said he was not sure if the Israeli prime minister would stand again.

item.title: 💰 Iran war anxiety doubles container shipping rates from Asia to US
item.description: The cost of shipping a container from Asia to the United States has doubled since the Iran war began, driven by soaring fuel prices and importers rushing to secure goods before costs rise further.

item.title: 🎯 Analysis suggests precision US strikes hit Iranian water facility
item.description: Satellite imagery and video analysis indicate US strikes may have hit a water treatment facility in Iran. It remains unclear whether the facility was deliberately targeted or struck inadvertently.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US launches fresh strikes on multiple targets in Iran as conflict escalates
item.description: US military conducted new airstrikes on Iranian targets as the Iran-US conflict entered a new phase. The strikes follow the downing of a US Apache helicopter and represent a significant escalation in hostilities.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes Russian-occupied Mariupol port, disrupting Crimean supply lines
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck the Russian-occupied port of Mariupol, Kyiv said Wednesday, the latest in a series of drone attacks on logistics connecting Russia to Crimea.

item.title: 🇰🇷 US human rights officials meet North Korea detainee families in Seoul
item.description: Senior US human rights officials met with families of Americans detained in North Korea during a visit to Seoul, highlighting ongoing diplomatic efforts on detainee issues.

item.title: 🇱🇧 UN to send investigators to Lebanon to assess potential war law violations
item.description: The United Nations will send a team to Lebanon to investigate potential violations of international law in the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese propagandists stoking dissent over Trump tariffs and data centres, OpenAI says
item.description: OpenAI has identified Chinese influence operations attempting to stoke dissent in the United States over Trump administration policies on tariffs and data centers.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Pentagon's expanded blacklist of Chinese firms tests Xi's US visit
item.description: The Pentagon's expanded blacklist of Chinese companies poses challenges for Xi Jinping's planned visit to the United States amid ongoing trade tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran declares closure of Hormuz, threatens to attack ships in strait
item.description: Iran declared the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and threatened to attack any vessels attempting to transit the critical waterway following the latest US strikes on Iranian territory.

item.title: 💥🇮🇷 Massive new US strikes rock multiple targets inside Iran
item.description: The United States launched a new wave of airstrikes targeting multiple locations inside Iran as the conflict between Washington and Tehran escalated sharply.

item.title: 💥🇮🇷 Explosions and airstrikes reported across southern Iran amid new US attacks
item.description: Multiple explosions and airstrikes were reported across southern Iran as the United States conducted new military operations against Iranian targets.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine police chief: Russia recruits young women to kill Ukrainian servicemen
item.description: Ukraine's police chief accused Russia of recruiting young women to carry out targeted killings of Ukrainian servicemen as part of a broader campaign of assassination and sabotage.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China-North Korea claim wins from Xi visit, but limits remain
item.description: China and North Korea hailed the results of Xi Jinping's visit as a diplomatic success, though analysts noted significant limitations remain in the bilateral relationship.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump sticks with Pulte for intel job as risk grows of lapse in spy powers
item.description: President Trump is standing by his nominee for a key intelligence position as the risk grows of a lapse in critical US surveillance authorities.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Conflict Escalates
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon according to Lebanese sources, as the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah continued to escalate.

item.title: 💰 US Inflation Hits Three-Year High at 4.2% as Iran War Drives Energy Prices Higher
item.description: US consumer prices rose at the fastest pace in three years, with inflation reaching 4.2% in May as energy prices surged amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: ☢️ UN Nuclear Watchdog Demands Iran Report Enriched Uranium Stocks
item.description: The IAEA Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution demanding Iran declare its enriched uranium stocks and allow inspectors to verify them.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan warns Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon now threaten Turkey
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan warned that Israel's ongoing attacks on Syria and Lebanon have reached a point where they now threaten Turkey's security as well.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine partners with Taurus missile maker for deep strike systems
item.description: Ukrainian defense company teams up with the maker of Taurus missiles to develop new deep strike capabilities amid ongoing conflict with Russia.

item.title: 🛢️ Countries vie to become energy safe haven as Hormuz crisis deepens
item.description: Multiple countries are positioning themselves as alternative energy suppliers as the crisis over the Strait of Hormuz threatens global oil supplies.

item.title: 🇮🇪🇪🇺 Ireland sets online safety, security priorities for EU presidency
item.description: Ireland announced online safety for children and security cooperation as key priorities for its upcoming EU presidency term.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇮🇪 Violent Unrest Erupts in Belfast Over Knife Attack Arrest
item.description: Violent unrest erupted in Belfast following the arrest of a suspect in a knife attack, highlighting ongoing tensions in Northern Ireland.
